_source: TH_EXPLORE_IT_DATA.json (authoritative structured data). Real accessions, per-item licence + open_access flag. Nothing authored by an assistant.

station.tagline: Same blood as her sister, raised outside the walls — and far more willing to tell you the truth.

museum_objects.title: HKMA Official 2010 Press Release
museum_objects.description: The Hong Kong Monetary Authority's official announcement establishing the formal offshore RMB trade settlement market.
museum_objects.significance: This press release is Wei's actual birth certificate — the day she was allowed to exist outside the walls that keep her sister, Mei, so carefully controlled.
museum_objects.plain_language: The official 2010 announcement from Hong Kong's monetary authority that created an offshore version of China's currency, free from mainland controls.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: HKMA 'Hong Kong as Offshore Renminbi Centre' Retrospective
museum_objects.description: A retrospective analysis by the HKMA on Hong Kong's development as the world's leading offshore renminbi trading hub.
museum_objects.significance: Four years after she was born, this document proves Wei had already made herself indispensable — the market's preferred way to bet on China without China's rules.
museum_objects.plain_language: A 2014 report explaining how Hong Kong became the world's main hub for trading this offshore version of Chinese currency.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: BIS Working Paper: CNH-CNY Pricing Dynamics
museum_objects.description: A Bank for International Settlements research paper analyzing the price relationship between onshore and offshore renminbi.
museum_objects.significance: Economists needed an entire research paper just to explain how two currencies with the same name can have two different prices — that's basically Wei's whole complicated relationship with her sister in one document.
museum_objects.plain_language: An academic paper explaining why the offshore and onshore versions of Chinese currency sometimes trade at different prices.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: Victoria Harbour / IFC Financial District Photographs
museum_objects.description: Photographs of Hong Kong's financial district skyline, home to the institutions that trade and manage the offshore renminbi market.
museum_objects.significance: This skyline is Wei's home turf — a place built specifically to let money move more freely than it's allowed to on the mainland.
museum_objects.plain_language: Photos of Hong Kong's famous financial district, home to the banks and traders who deal in this offshore Chinese currency.

resolved.warning: TERTIARY SOURCE — acceptable as a pointer, NOT as TH's evidence. Replace with the primary source it cites.

museum_objects.rights_note: NO OPEN IMAGE FOUND — panel must show text/citation only.

museum_objects.title: 2020 Hong Kong National Security Law Documentation
museum_objects.description: The controversial law imposed by Beijing on Hong Kong, raising questions about the territory's financial autonomy.
museum_objects.significance: For the first time since her birth, Wei's whole reason for existing — being separate from the mainland's rules — came under real threat.
museum_objects.plain_language: A controversial 2020 law that raised big questions about how independent Hong Kong's financial system could remain from mainland China.

resolved.warning: TERTIARY SOURCE — acceptable as a pointer, NOT as TH's evidence. Replace with the primary source it cites.

museum_objects.rights_note: CITED: the original object (rights unclear/blocked). DISPLAYED: an openly-licensed image of the same kind of object, credited to its own institution. world_map.map_description: Wei was born in Hong Kong's unique financial autonomy, growing from personal RMB services in 2003 into a full offshore trading market by 2010.

nodes.label: Hong Kong (HKMA)
nodes.description: Where the offshore renminbi trade settlement market was formally established in 2010.

nodes.label: Victoria Harbour
nodes.description: Iconic harbor at the heart of Hong Kong's financial district.

nodes.label: IFC / HSBC Building
nodes.description: Major financial institutions central to offshore renminbi trading and settlement.

nodes.label: Bank of China Tower
nodes.description: A key institution bridging mainland Chinese and Hong Kong financial systems.

nodes.label: Shanghai (onshore RMB)
nodes.description: Home of the onshore renminbi (Mei), whose tightly controlled exchange rate contrasts with Wei's freer offshore trading.
